title: Workflow — design event-driven order processing system from scratch
code_snippet:
# Goal: build event-driven order processing
# Services: order-service (producer), payment-service (consumer), notification-service (consumer)
# Events: order.created, order.paid, order.shipped
# Requirement: per-order ordering, at-least-once, schema evolution support
# Throughput: ~500 orders/sec peak
# Kafka 3.4 with Confluent Schema Registry
expected_feedback: Workflow: event-driven order processing design. (1) Topics: order.events (all order lifecycle events), 12 partitions (2x peak consumer count), RF=3, min.insync.replicas=2, 7-day retention. (2) Partition key: order_id (per-order ordering guaranteed). (3) Schema: Avro with envelope pattern (event_id, event_type, timestamp, source, correlation_id, payload), BACKWARD_TRANSITIVE compatibility in Schema Registry. (4) Producer: acks=all, enable.idempotence=true, outbox pattern for DB+event atomicity. (5) Consumers: payment-service and notification-service in separate consumer groups (fan-out), manual commit, idempotent processing, DLQ per consumer group. (6) Monitoring: consumer lag per partition, DLQ rate, producer error rate.
